lesson.heroSubtitle: AI is all around you. Let's find out what it really is!
lesson.explanation: Artificial Intelligence, or AI, is when computers are programmed to do things that normally need human intelligence. Things like understanding language, recognizing faces, playing games, and making decisions. AI is not magic — it learns from data, just like you learn from experience!

keyPoints.detail: Made by humans, not natural. Like artificial flowers vs real flowers.
The ability to learn, understand, and solve problems.
A computer system that can learn and make decisions like a human.

examples.text: Siri and Alexa understand your voice — that is AI.
Netflix recommends shows you might like — that is AI.
Google Translate translates languages — that is AI.
Face unlock on your phone recognizes you — that is AI.
Chess computers that beat humans — that is AI.
Self-driving cars that steer themselves — that is AI.

reminder.label: Key Idea
reminder.title: AI learns from data
reminder.body: Just like you get better at soccer by practicing, AI gets better by processing millions of examples. The more data it sees, the smarter it gets!

lesson.teacherNote: This lesson introduces AI as a concept Leo already experiences daily. Connect it to things he uses — his phone, YouTube recommendations, game AI opponents.

levelUp.miniLessonContent: AI is not just on your phone. It is being used in hospitals to detect diseases, in schools to personalize learning, in sports to analyze player performance, and even in art to create music and paintings. The future belongs to people who understand AI — and you are already learning it!

categories.label: AI in Sports
items: AI analyzes a soccer player's movements to improve their technique.
AI predicts which team will win based on statistics.

categories.label: AI in Medicine
items: AI scans X-rays and detects diseases faster than doctors.
AI helps design new medicines by testing millions of combinations.

categories.label: AI in Education
items: AI personalizes lessons based on what you already know.
AI tutors can answer questions 24 hours a day.

categories.label: AI in Creativity
items: AI can compose music, write stories, and create artwork.
AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT can have conversations and help you learn.

quiz.question: Which of these is an example of AI?
How does AI get smarter?
options: By learning from data and examples

quiz.question: Which is NOT an example of AI?
What is true about AI?
options: AI learns from data just like humans learn from experience

quiz.question: Which AI tool can have a conversation and help you learn?
AI in sports can:
options: Analyze player movements to improve technique

quiz.question: Why is learning about AI important for the future?
options: AI will replace all jobs tomorrow
The future belongs to people who understand AI
Only scientists need to know about AI

game.instructions: Match the AI example to the right category!
